title: Recovery System
subtitle: Mixed-signal recovery avionics board with redundant safing, voting, sensing, and ignition-driver control
stackup: Six copper-layer mixed-signal avionics PCB with high-current recovery-driver routing and separated logic/power regions
summary: A Yellow Jacket Space Program recovery PCB with dual-harness battery feeds, redundant STM32 flight channels, SPI IMU/barometer sensing, discrete median voting, four independent safing paths, buck-converter power stages, and ten ignition-driver channels for deployment control.
mainConstraints: Maintain redundant-domain isolation across three STM32/EKF channels
Minimize high di/dt hot-loop and return-path inductance around buck converters and ignition drivers
Separate high-current switching regions from low-voltage control and sensing circuitry
Preserve four independent safing paths through RBF, ESTOP, and software controls
Validate deployment loads, rail transients, voting behavior, and sensor interfaces during bring-up
publicNote: This portfolio entry is derived from the exported Altium schematic PDF. It does not parse or include raw .SchDoc or .PcbDoc files.

featured.description: Input protection, power-path control, and regulated rails for recovery electronics.

pages.title: Exported Schematic PDF

tests.name: Power Input and Rail Bring-up
tests.purpose: Verify VIN1/VIN2 behavior, 24 V rail, 3.3 V rail, and voltage-sense feedback before enabling recovery drivers.
tests.setup: Use current-limited bench supplies on each input path; probe P24V0, P3V3, 24V-VSNS, and 3V3-VSNS.
tests.expected: Rails sequence correctly, voltage sense nodes match divider expectations, and no abnormal current draw is observed.
tests.actual: Not yet documented in the provided PDF.
tests.notes: The portfolio should be updated with real scope captures and measured rail values after board bring-up.

tests.name: Voting Logic Functional Check
tests.purpose: Confirm the 1-fault-tolerant recovery enable voting behavior before connecting deployment loads.
tests.setup: Drive redundant microcontroller enable combinations and monitor RECO enable, latch, select, sense, and fault outputs.
tests.expected: Deployment outputs only enable for valid voting combinations and remain inhibited by ESTOP/RBF disable inputs.
tests.actual: Not yet documented in the provided PDF.
tests.notes: Add a truth table and logic-analyzer capture once tested.

tests.name: Recovery Driver Current Limit
tests.purpose: Validate constant-current behavior and fault reporting for recovery driver channels.
tests.setup: Use an electronic load or representative deployment load; command each driver bank and measure current, VREF feedback, and fault pins.
tests.expected: Driver current behavior matches the schematic notes, including current limit, 1 V/A gain, and fault handling.
tests.actual: Not yet documented in the provided PDF.
tests.notes: Document thermal behavior and channel-to-channel consistency.
